skill.name: skill-installer
skill.description: Guide Codex through listing curated skills and installing skills from GitHub repositories into the local Codex skills directory.
skill.summary: Installs Codex skills from curated OpenAI lists or GitHub repository paths.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static shell-execution and weak-crypto findings are mostly false positives from markdown command examples and ordinary prose. Real medium-risk behavior remains because the skill instructs agents to download skills from GitHub, write into the local Codex skills directory, and optionally use Git credentials or GitHub tokens for private repositories.

medium_findings.title: Remote Skill Installation Can Modify Agent Behavior
medium_findings.description: The skill installs remote GitHub content into $CODEX_HOME/skills and says users can overwrite preinstalled system skills if they insist. This is legitimate installer behavior, but it can change future agent instructions and should be treated as a supply-chain risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly describes installing GitHub-sourced skills into the Codex skills directory. No malicious repository is hardcoded, but the behavior has clear security impact.

medium_findings.title: Private Repository Installs May Use Git Credentials
medium_findings.description: The skill allows private GitHub repository access through existing git credentials or optional GITHUB_TOKEN and GH_TOKEN values. This is expected for private installs, but it raises credential-handling risk if helper implementations are unsafe.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token names and private-repository behavior are explicitly documented. I did not find evidence in SKILL.md that credentials are exfiltrated.

medium_findings.title: Sandbox Escalation Requested For Network Scripts
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents to request escalation when running its network helper scripts. This may be reasonable for network access, but marketplace users should know the workflow can require elevated execution approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The escalation instruction is present, but the referenced helper scripts are not included in the scanned package. The risk depends on the runtime environment and script implementation.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Triggered Execution Heuristics
low_findings.description: The external-command detections are markdown references to script paths and command options, not executable code in the scanned file. This reduces the command-injection concern for this package, although the intended workflow still involves running helper scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is visibly markdown prose and inline code spans. No executable source file or shell wrapper was present in the scanned file structure.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Point To GitHub Skill Sources
low_findings.description: The network findings identify GitHub URLs used to describe curated and system skill locations. These URLs are expected for a skill installer and do not show a hidden exfiltration endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All detected URLs are GitHub locations related to skill listing or installation. No evidence found of an unrelated collection server.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The lines flagged as weak cryptography contain metadata, installation prose, destination behavior, or option names. No cryptographic algorithm use was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not mention MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any other cryptographic operation. This appears to be a scanner heuristic mismatch.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Download Plus Local Skill Directory Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow combines GitHub downloads with writes under $CODEX_HOME/skills. That combination is powerful because installed skills can affect later agent behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network and filesystem behaviors are explicit in the file. The danger is contextual rather than confirmed malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Optional Credential Use During Remote Fetch
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill permits using existing git credentials or GITHUB_TOKEN and GH_TOKEN values for private repository access. Credential use should be limited to trusted repositories and reviewed helper scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential sources are named directly. No evidence found that the file instructs leaking or logging those credentials.

content.value_statement: Finding and installing Codex skills can require manual GitHub navigation and local setup. This skill guides Codex through listing curated skills and installing selected repositories.
actual_capabilities: Lists curated skills from the OpenAI skills repository.
Installs a named curated skill when the user selects one.
Installs skills from a GitHub repository path or tree URL.
Supports public repositories and private repositories with available credentials.
Places installed skills under the configured Codex skills directory.
Explains that Codex should restart after a successful install.
limitations: The scanned package contains only SKILL.md and references helper scripts that are not included.
It depends on network access to GitHub for listing and installation.
Private repository installs require existing git credentials or a GitHub token.
It does not assess the security quality of skills before installation.

use_cases.title: Install Curated Skills
use_cases.description: A developer asks Codex to show available curated skills and install selected items into the local skills directory.

use_cases.title: Add Team Skills From GitHub
use_cases.description: A team lead installs an internal skill from a private repository using existing GitHub access.

use_cases.title: Set Up A New Workspace
use_cases.description: A platform engineer prepares a Codex environment with the same approved skills used by the team.

prompt_templates.title: List Available Skills
prompt_templates.prompt: List the curated Codex skills that I can install.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request to discover available skills.

prompt_templates.title: Install One Curated Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the curated skill named skill-name and tell me when I need to restart Codex.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic installation after choosing a known skill.

prompt_templates.title: Install From Repository Path
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the Codex skill from owner/repository at path/to/skill. Use the main branch unless I specify otherwise.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate installation from a public GitHub repository.

prompt_templates.title: Install Multiple Team Skills
prompt_templates.prompt: Install these skills from our private GitHub repository paths, using my existing git credentials and stopping if any destination already exists.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced installation for controlled team setup.

output: Skills from the curated OpenAI skills repository:
Which ones would you like installed?

output_examples.output: Installed the selected skill into your Codex skills directory. Restart Codex to pick up new skills.

output_examples.input: Install a skill from a private repository.
output_examples.output: The install requires GitHub access for the private repository. I will use available git credentials or a GitHub token only for the requested repository.

best_practices: Install only skills from repositories you trust and can review.
Check whether the destination skill directory already exists before installing.
Restart Codex after installation so the new skill is loaded.
anti_patterns: Do not install skills from unknown repositories without review.
Do not expose broad GitHub tokens when a repository-specific credential is enough.
Do not overwrite preinstalled skills unless you have a clear rollback plan.

faq.question: What does this skill install?
faq.answer: It installs Codex skills from a curated OpenAI list or from a GitHub repository path.

faq.question: Does it support private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use existing git credentials or optional GitHub tokens for private repository access.

faq.question: Where are skills installed?
faq.answer: The skill describes installation into $CODEX_HOME/skills, with ~/.codex/skills as the default location.

faq.question: Does it validate installed skills for security?
faq.answer: No. Users should review each source repository before installing a skill.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: It downloads remote content, writes into the local skills directory, and may use credentials for private repositories.

faq.question: What should I do after installation?
faq.answer: Restart Codex so it can discover and load the installed skills.
